name: Moosehead Lake
description:
Moosehead Lake is Maine's largest lake and the largest mountain lake in the eastern United States, stretching 40 miles through the remote Maine North Woods. The lake's cold, deep water and wilderness setting create one of New England's premier cold-water fisheries.

Landlocked salmon are the signature species, with fish commonly reaching 3-5 pounds and trophy specimens exceeding 8 pounds. Lake trout patrol the deep water and can exceed 20 pounds. Brook trout inhabit the many inlet streams and bays. Greenville serves as the primary gateway, with several launches providing access to the lake's vast waters.

name: Sebago Lake
description:
Sebago Lake is Maine's second-largest and deepest lake, located just 30 miles northwest of Portland. The lake is the historic origin of the landlocked Atlantic salmon, which were first described here.

Landlocked salmon fishing is a spring and fall tradition, with ice-out in May triggering a legendary bite. Lake trout grow to impressive sizes in the cold depths, with fish over 20 pounds caught regularly. The warm-water fishery for smallmouth bass is excellent during summer. Multiple state and town ramps provide convenient access from the Portland area.

name: Rangeley Lake
description:
Rangeley Lake is the centerpiece of the famous Rangeley Lakes region in western Maine's mountains, a historic destination that helped launch American sport fishing in the 19th century. The lake and surrounding chain of ponds and rivers offer exceptional cold-water fishing.

Landlocked salmon provide the premier fishery, with spring trolling at ice-out being the most productive period. Brook trout in the inlet streams can reach trophy proportions. The lake's remote mountain setting at 1,520 feet elevation keeps water temperatures cold and fish active. The town of Rangeley provides launch access and full services.

name: Penobscot Bay
description:
Penobscot Bay is one of Maine's largest bays, a deep-water estuary where the Penobscot River meets the Atlantic Ocean. The bay's island-studded waters and strong tidal currents create productive fishing grounds.

Striped bass have expanded their range into the bay, providing excellent catch-and-release fishing during summer. Mackerel and pollock offer consistent action around rocky ledges and islands. The bay's deep water holds cod and pollock accessible to small boats. Multiple harbors and launch ramps from Belfast to Rockland provide access.

name: Casco Bay
description:
Casco Bay extends from Cape Elizabeth to Small Point, dotted with over 200 islands and forming the coastline just outside Portland. The bay provides the most accessible saltwater fishing from Maine's largest city.

Striped bass fishing has become world-class, with fish commonly exceeding 30 pounds during the summer migration. Bluefish provide exciting surface action, and mackerel schools attract larger predators throughout the bay. The numerous islands create current rips and eddies that concentrate baitfish and game fish. Portland's waterfront and surrounding harbors offer numerous launch options.

name: Kennebec River
description:
The Kennebec River flows 170 miles from Moosehead Lake to the Atlantic at Bath, providing both freshwater and saltwater fishing opportunities. Dam removals have restored migratory fish runs and rejuvenated the river's ecology.

Striped bass push up the tidal river from spring through fall, providing exciting fishing near Augusta and Gardiner. Smallmouth bass fishing above the tidal zone is excellent, with the river producing fish over 4 pounds. The restored alewife and shad runs attract predators and improve the entire ecosystem. Multiple state launches provide access throughout the river corridor.

name: Sebec Lake
description:
Sebec Lake is a scenic lake in central Maine's Piscataquis County, offering a mix of cold-water and warm-water fishing. The lake's moderate depth and good water quality support both landlocked salmon and quality smallmouth bass.

Landlocked salmon fishing is productive during spring and fall when water temperatures are optimal. Smallmouth bass provide excellent summer fishing along the rocky shoreline and around the lake's islands. The relatively uncrowded conditions and charming lakeside communities make it a favorite of Maine anglers seeking a quieter experience. A public launch provides boat access.

name: Grand Lake
description:
Grand Lake is one of the largest lakes in eastern Maine, part of the West Branch of the St. Croix River watershed near the Canadian border. The remote, lightly developed lake offers outstanding fishing and a true wilderness experience.

Smallmouth bass fishing is legendary, with the lake producing exceptional numbers of quality fish. Grand Lake Stream, the outlet, is a famous landlocked salmon and brook trout fishery that draws fly fishermen from across the country. The remote location limits access but rewards those who make the journey. The village of Grand Lake Stream provides launch access and guide services.
